task_completion.description: Create a back-of-house storage area enclosed by at least two interior partition walls and connected by at least one interior door.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan or 3D view, look for newly drawn interior walls that form an enclosed room separate from the main floor, with at least one door placed in these new walls.

task_completion.description: Define at least two distinct room areas with unique floor colors or textures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D plan or 3D view for at least two distinct zones (e.g., the main retail floor and the back-of-house storage) that have visibly different floor colors or patterns.

task_completion.description: Arrange at least 12 shelving units to form grocery aisles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Count the shelving units in the 2D plan, 3D view, or the left-hand furniture list. There should be 12 or more shelves arranged in parallel rows to create aisles.

task_completion.description: Create at least three checkout stations using counters and chairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least three distinct checkout areas near the front of the store, each consisting of a counter/desk and a chair.

task_completion.description: Place at least four refrigerators against the walls.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least four refrigerator units positioned along the perimeter walls of the retail space in the 2D plan or 3D view.

task_completion.description: Add at least two dimension lines measuring the width between aisles or checkout lanes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for dimension lines (lines with measurement numbers in the middle) drawn in the 2D plan specifically spanning the gaps between shelving units or checkout counters.

task_completion.description: The layout contains a minimum of 30 furniture items in total and the file is saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Check the furniture list panel on the left side of the screen; it should be heavily populated (at least 30 items). Also, look for evidence of saving, such as clicking 'File > Save' or the absence of an unsaved indicator in the window title.

integrity.description: The agent used the Sweet Home 3D graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application open with the agent actively using the mouse to place walls, rooms, furniture, and dimensions.

integrity.description: The agent built the layout progressively rather than loading a pre-made completed file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Sequential screenshots should show the layout evolving step-by-step (e.g., empty shell -> walls added -> furniture placed -> dimensions added).

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal scripts or code editors to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows or text editors (like VS Code or nano) should be visible modifying the .sh3d file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires modifying the starter file 'neighborhood_market_starter.sh3d' in Sweet Home 3D. The final layout must contain: at least two new interior walls and one interior door for a back-of-house area, at least two distinct rooms with unique floor colors/textures, at least 12 shelving units forming aisles, at least 3 checkout stations (counters + chairs), at least 4 refrigerators against walls, at least two dimension lines measuring aisle/checkout widths, and a total of at least 30 furniture items. Total points: 100. Pass threshold: 70.
